label: Self-organizing maps & competitive learning

node.label: Self-organizing maps & competitive learning
node.definition: A self-organizing map uses competitive learning to place prototype vectors on a low-dimensional lattice that approximately preserves neighborhood relationships.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Self-organizing maps & competitive learning is modeled as depending on Unsupervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
examples: atlas downstream link to Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Unsupervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: Self-organizing maps & competitive learning follows the learning setup expressed by Unsupervised learning.
In this atlas graph, Self-organizing maps & competitive learning has a `represents_with` dependency on Embeddings & distributed representations with `core` strength.
In this atlas graph, Self-organizing maps & competitive learning has a `trained_under` dependency on Perceptron, Hebbian, Oja & spike-timing plasticity with `common` strength.
In this atlas graph, Self-organizing maps & competitive learning has a `requires` dependency on Geometry, topology & symmetry with `common` strength.
